LAW AND MOTION 302, ARGUED AND THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: OVERRULED AS TO 1ST, 3RD, 4TH, 5TH AND 7TH CAUSES OF ACTION. DEMURRER SUSTAINED WITH 10 DAYS LEAVE TO AMEND AS TO REMAINDER. AS TO THE 2ND CAUSE OF ACTION (UNFAIR COMPETITION) AND 6TH CAUSE OF ACTION (INTENTIONAL INTERFERENCE WITH PROSPECTIVE ECONOMIC ADVANTAGE) PLAINTIFF MUST PLEAD FACTS SHOWING THE SOLICITATION OF PLAINTIFF'S EMPLOYEES WAS ACCOMPANIED BY UNLAWFUL ACTION. THE PLEADING SETS FORTH THE WRONGFUL CONDUCT IN CONCLUSORY TERMS WITHOUT FACTS SUPPORTING THOSE ALLEGATIONS. ALTHOUGH THE 2ND CAUSE OF ACTION DOES ALLEGE FRAUD AND THEFT OF TRADE SECRETS, IF "STEALING MC SQUARED EMPLOYEES" IS TO CONSTITUTE AN UNFAIR BUSINESS PRACTICE, ADDITIONAL FACTS MUST BE STATED IN SUPPORT. PLAINTIFF SUFFICIENTLY ALLEGES AN EXISTING ECONOMIC RELATIONSHIP TO SUPPORT THE 6TH CAUSE OF ACTION BUT MUST STATE A WRONGFUL ACT APART FROM THE INTERFERENCE ITSELF. PLAINTIFF TO SUBMIT A PROPOSED FORM OF ORDER. JUDGE: CHARLOTTE WALTER WOOLARD, COURT REPORTER: KENT GUBBINE, CSR #5797

SUMMONS ON COMPLAINT FILED BY PLAINTIFF MC SQUARED HOLDINGS INC., A CALIFORNIA AND COLORADO CORPORATION SERVED JAN-12-2010, SUBSTITUTE SERVICE ON NATURAL PERSON AS TO DEFENDANT NSA WIRELESS INC., A CALIFORNIA CORPORATION

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT NSA WIRELESS, INC. DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT. NO APPEARANCES ON THIS MATTER. THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: DEMURRER OVERRULED IN PART AND SUSTAINED IN PART. DEMURRER OVERRULED AS TO 1ST, 4TH, 6TH, AND 8TH CAUSES OF ACTION. WHETHER B&P CODE SECTION 7031(A) PRECLUDES MC SQUARED FROM SEEKING COMPENSATION FOR SERVICES RENDERED IS A FACTUAL ISSUE NOT APPROPRIATE FOR DEMURRER. DEMURRER SUSTAINED WITH TEN DAYS LEAVE TO AMEND AS TO 3RD (UNFAIR COMPETITION), 5TH (INTENTIONAL MISREPRESENTATION), AND 7TH (INTENTIONAL INTERFERENCE WITH PROSPECTIVE ECONOMIC ADVANTAGE) CAUSES OF ACTION. PLAINTIFF TO PLEAD MISREPRESENTATION WITH SPECIFICITY, AND ALLEGE WHAT PARTICULAR PRACTICES OF DEFENDANT ARE UNFAIR PURSUANT TO B&P CODE SECTION 17200. TO SUPPORT A CLAIM OF INTENTIONAL INTERFERENCE, PLAINTIFF MUST ALLEGE AN EXISTING ECONOMIC RELATIONSHIP THAT DEFENDANT INTERFERED WITH. SEE ROTH VS. RHODES (1994) 25 CAL. APP. 4TH 530, 546. DEMURRER SUSTAINED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND AS TO 2ND CAUSE OF ACTION (BREACH OF IMPLIED COVENANT) WHICH DOES NOT GIVE RISE TO AN ACTION IN TORT EXCEPT IN THE CONTEXT OF AN INSURANCE CONTRACT. SUTHERLAND VS. BARCLAYS AMERICAN/MORTGAGE CORP. (1997) 53 CAL. APP. 4TH 299, 314. (MOVING PARTY TO PREPARE A FORM OF ORDER.) JUDGE: CHARLOTTE WALTER WOOLARD; COURT REPORTER: KENT GUBBINE, CSR #5797

DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jul-20-2010 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANT NSA WIRELESS, INC'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT. Appearances: Joseph L. Urbanski of Bradshaw & Associates (433.4800) for plaintiff and responding party; Fredrick A. Hagen (925.838.2090) for defendant and responding party. Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: overruled as to 1st, 3rd, 4th, 5th and 7th causes of action. Demurrer sustained with 10 days leave to amend as to remainder. As to the 2nd cause of action (unfair competition) and 6th cause of action (intentional interference with prospective economic advantage) plaintiff must plead facts showing the solicitation of plaintiff's employees was accompanied by unlawful action. The pleading sets forth the wrongful conduct in conclusory terms without facts supporting those allegations. Although the 2nd cause of action does allege fraud and theft of trade secrets, if "stealing MC Squared employees" is to constitute an unfair business practice, additional facts must be stated in support. Plaintiff sufficiently alleges an existing economic relationship to support the 6th cause of action but must state a wrongful act apart from the interference itself. Plaintiff to submit a proposed form of order. (302/CWW) Judge: Charlotte Walter Woolard, Court Reporter: Kent Gubbine, CSR #5797

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar May-17-2010 Continued To Jul-16-10 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 212

DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Mar-29-2010 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANT NSA WIRELESS, INC. DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT. No appearances on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: Demurrer overruled in part and sustained in part. Demurrer overruled as to 1st, 4th, 6th, and 8th causes of action. Whether B&P Code section 7031(a) precludes MC Squared from seeking compensation for services rendered is a factual issue not appropriate for demurrer. Demurrer sustained with ten days leave to amend as to 3rd (unfair competition), 5th (Intentional Misrepresentation), and 7th (Intentional Interference with Prospective Economic Advantage) causes of action. Plaintiff to plead misrepresentation with specificity, and allege what particular practices of defendant are unfair pursuant to B&P Code section 17200. To support a claim of Intentional Interference, plaintiff must allege an existing economic relationship that defendant interfered with. See Roth vs. Rhodes (1994) 25 Cal. App. 4th 530, 546. Demurrer sustained without leave to amend as to 2nd cause of action (Breach of Implied Covenant) which does not give rise to an action in tort except in the context of an insurance contract. Sutherland vs. Barclays American/Mortgage Corp. (1997) 53 Cal. App. 4th 299, 314. (Moving party to prepare a form of order.) (302/CWW) Judge: Charlotte Walter Woolard; Court Reporter: Kent Gubbine, CSR #5797
